@flying_fisher some hopefully relevant paraphrases about the relationship between state, capital, property (from john holloway's change the world without taking power):

@flying_fisher - ("Capital dominates others by owning the 'done'")
- ("Capital robs and the state supplies the weapons")
- ("Capital breaks the collective 'we' into 'I / you / he / she / they'")
- ("Capitalism freed exploitation from national limitations by abstracting the worker through money"

@flying_fisher - ("Marx: capitalism inverses relationships to the point where workers become the instruments of money, capital and machines; social relations are between things (money and the state, your money and mine) whereas human 'individuals' are merely there to exchange commodities")

@flying_fisher - ("Capital is an inherently mobile form of domination: if it doesn't like the workers it subordinates, it can eject them from the premises, dissolve itself into money, and find more subordinate, more 'flexible' workers")

@flying_fisher - ("Representative democracy ensures that the masses want what the bourgeoisie thinks they should want. The masses are included in a form of articulation that excludes them. It is not a victory against capitalist rule, it is an extension of capital's domination")

@flying_fisher - ("Capital is the movement of denying movement")
- ("The more capital is repelled by people, the more violently it tries to enclose them by extending the notion of property (genes, software, land) and refashioning people in its own image")
@flying_fisher - ("Establishing property and seperating people from the land as a means of doing or self-sustainability leaves them with no choice but to freely choose to be exploited")
@flying_fisher - ("The system of property, like a maze with no exit, eventually funnels people back into the labour market to work as wage slaves.")

1. a thread of splurts about #ZeroData to better articulate benefits and trade-offs from my earlier conversation with @rafajkennedy https://t.co/GQ4iT1RIl2

2. #ZeroData app development is simpler because there's no need to build, secure, maintain account infrastructure. people bring their own storage and identity, and your app plugs into that. the developer can put more energy into features and improvements.

3. most of the #ZeroData integrations are to read and write data, letting the library handle syncing and other things. this fundamental simplicity means protocols are swappable, and an app can participate in a larger pool of potential users.

4. instead of dealing with network calls or REST, a #ZeroData app will just `await` and the library will act on local or elsewhere, which facilitates local-first https://t.co/hN2UjIi1gJ or offline-first https://t.co/ZGy9XyzJtS experiences.

5. browser apps (or edge apps) don't need a server for dynamic requests. hosting your site statically, is very affordable and easy to mirror. #ZeroData apps organized this way obviate 'self-hosting' because it doesn't change the data story much, you already #OwnYourData.

6. (for when self-hosting does make a difference, there are some great solutions like @cloudron_io @cap_rover and @yunohost that simplify server admin to "click button, get app") https://t.co/BbIQcvgDbc

7. not requiring an identity enables doorless apps. like most internet content, it's freely shareable and easy to consume—there is no 'install process'. #ZeroData is a great way to unlock the commons https://t.co/SDcGQZ6ReR
8. check out the growing set of over a hundred #ZeroData apps that work this way https://t.co/Mop28FTu8A

9. at the moment, many #ZeroData apps seem to fit 'tools for individuals' better that 'collaborative, aggregated, multi-user', but hopefully this will be solved with time. @inkandswitch may have some contributions with 'backchannel' https://t.co/hy9Zz8PqwR

10. there aren't obvious patterns for funding #ZeroData apps. possibilities might include patronage, donations, memberships, ads, swag… verifiable credentials via cryptography might be a way to do content-gating while avoiding centralized accounts.

11. protocol differences? i think @remotestorage_ for traditional technology, @FISSIONcodes for passwordless end-to-end encrypted + stored on @IPFS, @project_solid for linked data to enable machine processing.

#ZeroData encourages interoperability to make the pie larger for all.

to change society, start by changing the way people think: share your perspectives, dialogue with others, understand those who think differently.

@haideralmosawi i don't find guilt useful as a motivator, prefer to just balance things out with all the other things a healthy life entails like relationships, movement, nature, water, oxygen, mental health, feeling fulfilled, etc…

@haideralmosawi but some specific food strategies i have used:

1) allowing myself to try whatever i'm curious about which can remove the taboo around consumption and thus make it kind of boring or predictable (ends when there aren't many new options or, more likely, i have no more money)
@haideralmosawi 2) "eat whatever i want as long as i make it myself", at least makes each ingredient a more conscious choice

@haideralmosawi 3) the 'kaizen' method of consciously eating unnoticable amounts less or more of something (one more green pea, one less nibble of chocolate), at regular intervals, choosing something absurd/'easy'  enough that your brain might not resist

@haideralmosawi but find whatever brings you to balance. the ultimate step for me is to 'do everything with consciousness', without any fail mode, just being deliberate in every action and observing as much as possible
